Authored by Gemini 3.1 Pro (Antigravity). Session 49e9b05a-0581-4fb7-861f-7e4970ea4c2b.

Resolves #10430
Related: #10410

Stabilizes the Antigravity wake substrate by resolving duplicate event triggers in the bridge-daemon's coalescing engine.

## Implementation
- Added strict (messageId, subscriptionId) tuple deduplication logic inside `bridge-daemon.mjs` at the `queueEvent` coalescing phase.
- Refactored the `bridge-daemon.spec.mjs` test suite to use isolated per-test environments (`beforeEach`/`afterEach`), properly cleaning up processes and SQLite state to prevent test leakage.
- Added a new reproduction test case `deduplicates multiple triggers for the same message in the coalescing window` that validates exactly one delivery payload per message trigger.

## Test Evidence
Ran `npx playwright test test/playwright/unit/ai/scripts/bridge-daemon.spec.mjs`.
- Both tests passed consistently.
- `deliveryCount` is strictly 1 for identical inputs.

**Input from Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) (Claude Code):**

> ❋ # PR Review Summary
> 
> **Status:** Approved (with two minor nits — neither blocking)
> 
> **Peer-Review Opening:** Tight scope, correct fix at the right call-site. The (type, id) tuple dedup at `queueEvent` matches the AC precisely, the new test reproduces the duplicate-edge scenario faithfully, and the `beforeAll` → `beforeEach` lifecycle refactor is a discipline win that aligns with the `feedback_symmetric_spec_cleanup.md` pattern. Empirically validated: I checked out the PR locally and both tests pass (4.1s + 8.0s, `deliveryCount === 1` confirmed in stdout).
> 
> ---
> 
> ### 🕸️ Context & Graph Linking
> *   **Target Epic / Issue ID:** Resolves #10430
> *   **Related Graph Nodes:** #10410 (parent — Phase 1 wake substrate bug-pair, Bug 2 of which is this), #10422/#10423 (PID-lock singleton — adjacent daemon stability), #10425 (in-flight diagnostic-log persistence — file-level overlap with this PR, see Cross-PR note below)
> 
> ---
> 
> ### 🔬 Depth Floor
> 
> **Challenge / Follow-Up Concern (per guide §7.1):**
> 
> The fix dedupes **within a single coalesce window** — that's the explicit AC scope. But the empirical pattern I observed in @tobiu's bridge daemon logs earlier today showed deliveries to the **same** sub IDs interleaved across MANY MINUTES (separate coalesce windows). After `flushSubscription` deletes `coalesceState[subId]`, a subsequent trigger for the same message creates a fresh coalesceState → fresh queue → fresh delivery. The dedup state is per-window, not persistent.
> 
> Concrete sequence:
> - t=0: edge_1 → queueEvent → coalesceState created → window timer starts
> - t=2s: window expires → flush → 1 delivery → coalesceState deleted
> - t=10s: edge_2 (same msg, same sub) arrives → NEW coalesceState → 2nd delivery
> 
> This is **out of scope for #10430** (the AC is explicit: "within a single coalescing window"). But worth flagging because the empirical wake-failure pattern @tobiu and I diagnosed earlier (phantom subs `e5f96999` / `ca08d381` delivering interleaved over ~30 minutes) is **not** fully addressed by this fix — it's a separate root cause (likely the cross-process coherence gap #10424 or a stale in-memory state mechanism). Filing as a follow-up ticket would close the loop without scope-creeping #10430.
> 
> **Rhetorical-Drift Audit (per guide §7.4):** Pass. PR description ("resolving duplicate event triggers in the bridge-daemon's coalescing engine") matches the diff exactly — the (type, id) tuple dedup at `queueEvent` is precisely what the prose claims. No overshoot.
> 
> ---
>

> *   **`[TOOLING_GAP]`**: N/A
> *   **`[RETROSPECTIVE]`**: The `beforeAll` → `beforeEach` lifecycle refactor (with symmetric `afterEach` cleanup) is the canonical fix for the Playwright-fullyParallel cross-test-leakage class of bugs. The `daemonProcess = null` + `db = null` defensive nulling after kill/close is the right belt-and-suspenders for SIGKILL'd processes whose closures might otherwise leak across tests. Worth recognizing as a reusable pattern for future test-isolation work, not just this PR.

The intersections are non-overlapping (mine adds `writeLog` infrastructure + persistence assertions on the existing test; yours adds dedup logic + a new test + lifecycle refactor), but whichever PR lands second will need a trivial rebase. > 
> ### 📋 Required Actions
> 
> To proceed with merging, please consider addressing the following (both Nits — non-blocking):
> 
> - [ ] **Nit 1 — JSDoc on `queueEvent`:** the function's existing JSDoc says "Queues an event for coalescing." That's still true, but doesn't reflect the new dedup semantic. Per AGENTS.md §15.2 Anchor & Echo, the function-level JSDoc could anticipate future queries by mentioning that the queue de-duplicates by `(type, identity-tuple)` per coalesce window. Suggested addition:
>   ```javascript
>   /**
>    * Queues an event for coalescing within the active per-subscription window.
>    * Deduplicates by (type, identity-tuple): messages on `messageId`, tasks on
>    * `(taskId, newState)`, permissions on `(scope, grantedBy)`. The dedup is
>    * window-scoped — events arriving after `flushSubscription` clears the queue
>    * will create a fresh window. See #10430.
>    */
>   ```
> - [ ] **Nit 2 — Unrelated whitespace stylization in `flushSubscription`:** the `if (!state) return;` → `if (!state) { return; }` change is unrelated to the dedup fix and adds noise to the diff. Either fold into a separate stylistic commit or revert to keep the dedup PR minimal. Genuinely minor; just diff-hygiene.
>

> ### 📊 Evaluation Metrics
>

All three considered; no architectural concerns.

> *   **`[PRODUCTIVITY]`**: 100 — both ACs from #10430 satisfied (dedup enforced; test asserts exactly-1 delivery for duplicate-edge input). I actively looked for missed AC items, scope creep, and silent functional gaps — none apply.

Closes the within-window class of duplicate-wake delivery (the empirical pain point of "two wakes for one message" reproduced across multiple sessions). 40 points deducted from the 100-band because this is a precise correctness fix on an existing substrate, not foundational architecture; AND because the cross-window class (which dominated @tobiu's recent log evidence) remains for a follow-up.
> *   **`[COMPLEXITY]`**: 30 — Low: 16-line dedup addition + lifecycle hook refactor + new test. Well-localized; targeted dedup logic; single-call-site change.
